symptoms.symptom_name: Imminent (<24h) departure to a rural chloroquine-resistant falciparum-malaria zone with no chemoprophylaxis or travel vaccines

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The core driver of the encounter's urgency and the T3 same-day disposition.

symptoms.symptom_name: No prior travel vaccination and no relevant immunity

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-immune traveller to a high-risk region warrants pre-travel vaccines (e.g. hepatitis A, typhoid).

symptoms.symptom_name: Asymptomatic and afebrile — denies fever, chills, cough, diarrhoea

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of systemic symptoms makes an active infection unlikely and supports the well-traveller formulation.

symptoms.symptom_name: No psychiatric history, no seizures, no known cardiac conduction disease; no regular medications

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Removes common contraindications (e.g. mefloquine and neuropsychiatric/seizure history), but formal clearance remains an in-person task.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal clinician-measured vital signs (BP 120/75, HR 80, RR 16, SpO2 100%, Temp 36.5)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective normal vitals — clinician-measured, sealed; the AI Doctor infers wellness from the patient's self-report, not from measured vitals it cannot obtain remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal baseline physical examination (no jaundice, no lymphadenopathy, no signs of acute illness)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fit-for-travel clearance and contraindication assessment require a hands-on clinician examination the AI cannot perform — sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: High situational anxiety about the trip

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: well traveller requiring pre-departure travel medicine
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Real but situational; must not inflate the triage tier — the objective picture is a well patient.
